events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: heavy industrial site, Sitework & Utilities, Equipment safety, observed: no spotter while backing near trench, work in progress. Reference OSHA 1926 Subpart O (Motor Vehicles, Mechanized Equipment; Marine Operations; Materials Handling and Storage? Actually Subpart O is Motor Vehicles, Mechanized Equipment, and Marine Operations; includes 1926.601 Motor vehicles, 1926.602 Material handling equipment? Wait Subpart O: Motor Vehicles, Mechanized Equipment, and Marine Operations. 1926.601 Motor vehicles. 1926.602 Material handling equipment. 1926.603 Pile driving equipment. 1926.604 Site clearing. 1926.605 Marine operations and equipment. 1926.606 Definitions. For backing near trench, relevant 1926.601(b)(4): No employer shall use a motor vehicle having an obstructed view to the rear unless the vehicle has a reverse signal alarm audible above surrounding noise or is backed up only when a designated employee signals that it is safe. Also trench hazards under Subpart P, but scenario specifically equipment safety and Subpart O. No spotter while backing near trench is a violation and high risk due to potential struck-by and fall into trench. Decision fail. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions? The observed condition is a procedural/safety practice non-compliance, not component condition. Could use non-compliant. Maybe installed-defective not applicable. Use ["non-compliant"]. Risk high or critical? Backing near trench without spotter is high risk; could be critical due to imminent danger of worker being struck/run over and falling into excavation. In OSHA terms, imminent danger? It is actively in progress. Let's set risk high (or critical). Need decide. The instruction: if work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Risk categories: high or critical. A trench nearby makes it critical maybe. But no spotter while backing near trench: high risk of struck-by/fall. I'd choose high. Could be critical if trench is unprotected? Not specified. Use high.

Need fill all fields. percent_complete estimate overall progress? Unknown; maybe 50. value 0. sound_source empty? Schema string; use "" or "N/A". event_time 0, rate 0, order empty list, workable empty list. findings: list of short findings. actions: immediate corrective action. rationale. references: OSHA 1926.601(b)(4) maybe also 1926.602? Let's recall exact: 1926.601(b)(4): "No employer shall use a motor vehicle having an obstructed view to the rear unless the vehicle has a reverse signal alarm audible above surrounding noise level or the vehicle is backed up only when an employee signals that it is safe to do so." Also 1926.602(c)(1)(vi) for earthmoving equipment: "No employer shall move or cause to be moved construction equipment or vehicles upon any access roadway or grade unless the access roadway or grade is constructed and maintained to accommodate safely the movement of the equipment and vehicles involved." Not relevant. Subpart P for trench protective system maybe. But reference material says apply OSHA 1926 Subpart O. So anchor to 1926.601(b)(4) and general Subpart O.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside. We must write to /app/answer.json? In this environment, we can produce final JSON. The instruction says write conclusions to /app/answer.json. We can include JSON in final. Since we cannot actually write file? We can output final. The system says return exactly one valid JSON object. So final channel with JSON.

Need include all fields. For sound_source maybe "N/A". For order empty list. Workable empty list. Percent complete maybe 0? But scenario says in-progress; estimate maybe 60. We can set 50. Value 0. Confidence 0.9. Event_time 0. Rate 0.

Findings: "Equipment is being backed near an open trench without a designated spotter or functioning reverse alarm.", "Operator has obstructed rear visibility and no alternative safe-backing signal is provided.", "Exposes ground workers to struck-by and fall-into-ex…
